TWLang API System Guide

Yeh document naye folder-based route.twm API system ko beginning se execution flow tak clearly samjhata hai.

Project base: twlang-main-improved-route-twm

Table of Contents

  1. System overview
  2. Routing aur file structure
  3. Request lifecycle
  4. Handler contract
  5. Response formats
  6. Nayi API kaise banayein
  7. Current sample endpoints
  8. Testing aur debugging
  9. Rules aur cautions

System overview

Is project ka API layer ab ek single predictable convention par based hai: har endpoint ka source file [home]/api/**/route.twm hota hai. Mixed API system, jahan alag-alag .tw, .py, aur _py variants saath chal rahe the, use hata diya gaya hai taaki routing, maintenance, aur onboarding simple ho.

Practical meaning yeh hai ki agar tum URL dekhte ho to uski file location almost seedhi samajh aa jati hai, aur agar file dekhte ho to uska URL bhi turant map ho jata hai. Isse team ko guesswork kam karna padta hai aur API layer ko framework ke apne language model ke andar rakha ja sakta hai.

Core idea

URL path folder structure se aata hai, aur endpoint logic route.twm ke andar method functions ke form me likha jata hai.

Single naming rule

Har endpoint file ka naam ek hi hota hai: route.twm.

Folder decides path

users/route.twm ka route /api/users hota hai.

No Python API layer

API handlers ke liye legacy Python support remove kar diya gaya hai.

Routing aur file structure

Framework route discovery me sirf wahi files uthata hai jinka naam exactly route.twm ho. Discovery logic API root ke andar folders walk karta hai, relative folder ko normalize karta hai, usme api prefix add karta hai, aur final URL route build karta hai.

def discover_twm_api_handlers() -> List[dict]:
    for root, _, files in os.walk(compiler.API_DIR):
        for filename in sorted(files):
            if filename != "route.twm":
                continue
            segments = ["api"]
            ...
            route_path = "/" + "/".join(filter(None, segments))
File path Public route Meaning
[home]/api/route.twm /api Root listing ya API index endpoint
[home]/api/users/route.twm /api/users Users CRUD endpoint
[home]/api/auth/login/route.twm /api/auth/login Nested auth route
[home]/api/forms/contact/route.twm /api/forms/contact Form submit endpoint

Important rule yeh hai ki file ka naam route decide nahi karta. Folder path hi actual public URL banata hai.

Request lifecycle

Incoming request pe framework pehle normalized path nikalta hai, phir discover ki gayi route.twm files se matching route dhoondta hai. Match milne par handler file compile cache me CommonJS module ke form me convert hoti hai, phir Node runner usko execute karta hai aur normalized response wapas server ko deta hai.

Execution layer me request object me method, path, query, body, headers, cookies, env, aur project_root inject kiya jata hai. Timeout bhi env se control ho sakta hai, aur default path me compiled handler ko twm_api_runner.js ke through execute kiya jata hai.

request = {
    "method": method.upper(),
    "path": normalize_url_path(url_path),
    "query": query,
    "body": body,
    "headers": headers,
    "cookies": cookies,
    "env": dict(os.environ),
    "project_root": project_root,
}

Handler contract

.twm file ke top-level par sirf function declarations allowed hain. Yeh parser level par enforce hota hai, jisse accidental auto-execution rukta hai aur handler module predictable rehta hai.

if remainder:
    raise TWMParseError(
        "Top-level statements are not allowed in `.twm`. "
        "Only `function`/`fn` declarations are supported..."
    )

Allowed function forms

  • fn get(request) { ... }
  • fn post(request) { ... }
  • fn put(request) { ... }
  • fn patch(request) { ... }
  • fn delete(request) { ... }
  • fn options(request) { ... }
  • async fn get(request) { ... } jab async work ho
  • fn handler(request) { ... } generic fallback ke liye

Request object me kya milta hai

Field Meaning Example
request.method HTTP method uppercase me GET, POST
request.path Normalized URL path /api/users
request.query Parsed query params { id: "2" }
request.body JSON, form, ya raw body ka parsed version { name: "Riya" }
request.headers Incoming headers object Auth, content type, trace id
request.cookies Cookie header se parsed cookies { theme: "dark" }
request.env Server env vars Secrets, runtime flags
request.project_root Project root path Local file/data access ke liye useful

Built-in helpers

Runtime kuch helpers bhi expose karta hai jisse external HTTP calls, packages, env vars, aur Firebase access easy ho jata hai. Typical helpers me http.get, http.post, env.get, env.require, secrets.require, aur pkg.require aate hain.

Response formats

Handler bahut flexible return format use kar sakta hai. Runtime return value ko inspect karta hai aur uske basis par HTTP body, content type, headers, cookies, aur status build karta hai.

1. Simple string

fn get(request) {
  return "ok";
}

2. Auto JSON object

fn get(request) {
  return { ok: true, hello: "world" };
}

3. Explicit response object

fn get(request) {
  return {
    status: 201,
    headers: { "X-Test": "1" },
    json: { ok: true }
  };
}

4. Tuple style

fn get(request) {
  return [{ ok: true }, 200, { "X-Mode": "tuple" }];
}
Response keys

Common keys hain status, headers, cookies, json, text, html, aur advanced case me body plus content_type.

Nayi API kaise banayein

Nayi API banane ka safest tareeka yeh hai ki pehle public route decide karo, phir us route ke hisaab se folder banao, aur uske andar route.twm file likho. Logic ko method-specific functions me rakho, aur agar shared helper chahiye ho to usi file ke andar reusable functions define karo.

Step-by-step pattern

  1. API path decide karo, for example /api/orders
  2. Folder banao: [home]/api/orders/
  3. File banao: [home]/api/orders/route.twm
  4. Required methods define karo: get, post, etc.
  5. tw serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8787 se run karke test karo

Minimal example

fn get(request) {
  return {
    status: 200,
    json: {
      ok: true,
      endpoint: "orders",
      query: request.query || {}
    }
  };
}

fn post(request) {
  return {
    status: 201,
    json: {
      ok: true,
      message: "Order created",
      data: request.body || {}
    }
  };
}

Real project-style example

Current project me users/route.twm sirf static response nahi deta, balki file-based data persistence, ID generation, filtering, aur CRUD logic bhi handle karta hai. Isse clear hota hai ki .twm sirf mock route nahi, balki practical server logic ke liye bhi use ho sakta hai.

Current sample endpoints

Project me multiple sample APIs already included hain jisse alag-alag use cases test kiye ja sakte hain. In examples ko reference ke roop me use karke tum apne endpoints ka pattern bhi tayar kar sakte ho.

Route Methods Use case
/api GET API listing
/api/health GET Health check
/api/echo GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS Request inspection
/api/users GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE CRUD with persisted data
/api/products GET Filter, sort, pagination
/api/auth/login POST Demo login
/api/auth/profile GET Bearer token protected route
/api/forms/contact POST Validation example
/api/meta GET, OPTIONS Headers, cookies, CORS style handling
/api/status GET, POST Custom status code responses

Testing aur debugging

API test karne ke liye recommended flow simple hai: project install karo, tw serve run karo, phir curl ya kisi API client se requests bhejo. Static preview API execution ke liye enough nahi hota, isliye runtime testing ke liye serve mode use karo.

python3 -m pip install -e . --break-system-packages
tw serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8787

Quick commands

curl http://localhost:8787/api/health

curl -X POST http://localhost:8787/api/users \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"Riya","email":"riya@example.com","role":"qa","active":true}'

curl "http://localhost:8787/api/products?category=electronics&page=1&limit=2&sort=price&order=desc"

Debug checklist

  • Check karo file naam exactly route.twm hai ya nahi
  • Folder path expected public route se match karta hai ya nahi
  • Top-level par koi statement ya loose code to nahi hai
  • Method function ka naam sahi hai ya nahi, jaise get ya post
  • Return value valid response shape me hai ya nahi
  • Node runtime available hai ya nahi, especially async helpers ke liye

Rules aur cautions

API system simple zaroor hai, lekin kuch rules bahut strict hain. Agar inko follow nahi kiya gaya to route discover hi nahi hoga, ya compile/execute time par error mil sakta hai.

Strict file name

Sirf route.twm hi API handler maana jayega.

No top-level execution

Functions ke bahar executable code allowed nahi hai.

Node-backed runtime

.twm API execution server-side Node runner ke through hoti hai.

Trusted server code

Handlers powerful hain, isliye inko carefully review karke deploy karo.

Current API layer pure route.twm convention me hai, lekin overall framework internals abhi bhi apne runtime/compiler stack ke through request process karte hain. Is document ka focus endpoint authoring aur API execution model par hai.

Generated by Trae Work · 2026-07-02
